---
title: "Temperature Converter"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Temperature Converter}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

A classic reactive UI challenge: two inputs representing the same value in different units, where editing either one should update the other. In traditional Shiny this requires careful coordination to avoid feedback loops. With nacre's controlled inputs, the solution is straightforward — one `reactiveVal` holds the canonical Celsius value, and the Fahrenheit input derives from it via a `reactive()`. Both the number inputs and the vertical sliders stay in sync automatically.

<iframe
  src="../../apps/temperature/"
  width="100%"
  height="520px"
  style="border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 4px;"
></iframe>

<details><summary>Show source</summary>

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
knitr::asis_output(paste0("```r\n", paste(readLines("../../examples/temperature.R"), collapse = "\n"), "\n```"))
```

</details>
